name: Does AEO software handle all the technical stuff like schema and robots, or will we still need technical SEO skills on our team?

mainEntity.name: Does AEO software handle all the technical stuff like schema and robots, or will we still need technical SEO skills on our team?

acceptedAnswer.text:
AEO software often includes basic checks for structured data and page structure, but you still need someone to implement fixes—adding FAQ schema, improving headings, and cleaning robots directives. Technical SEO fundamentals remain necessary; the software points to issues but doesn’t automatically rewrite your site.

That said, many tools guide non-technical users through common improvements with templates and recommendations.

Key factors:
- Extent of built-in diagnostics vs auto-fix capabilities
- Your team’s comfort with CMS and markup changes
- Complexity of your current site architecture
- Support or documentation provided by the AEO vendor

Plan for at least light technical involvement: a developer or technically minded marketer should own implementation. Use the software’s reports as a prioritized backlog for changes.

Frame AEO tooling as amplifying, not replacing, essential technical hygiene on your site.
acceptedAnswer.description: AEO tools can flag schema, structure, and robots issues, but they don’t fully replace technical SEO skills. You’ll still need someone to implement changes in your CMS and markup, using the software’s diagnostics as a prioritized backlog of fixes and improvements.
